title: CodeGraph CLI `explain --json` output
description: Single-edge resolution trace. Surfaces extractor provenance, resolver strategy, confidence, raw metadata, and source/target node snapshots. The edgeId field is index-local (resets on rebuild) — round-trip stability requires the canonical (source, target, kind, line, col) identity instead.

extractorProvenance.description: edges.provenance column — which extractor laid down the reference (tree-sitter | scip | heuristic). Null on v5 rows or synthesized edges that didn't carry an extractor tag.

resolvedBy.description: metadata.resolvedBy strategy tag (import | framework | qualified-name | exact-match | instance-method | file-path | fuzzy). Null when the edge has no resolver metadata.

confidence.description: metadata.confidence in [0, 1]. Null when no confidence was persisted.

metadata.description: Decoded metadata JSON when it was a plain object. Null when absent OR when metadata was a non-object value — see rawMetadata for that case.

rawMetadata.description: Raw JSON text of the metadata column when it parses to a non-object value (array, scalar, malformed). Null when metadata is absent or a clean object (then `metadata` carries it).

traceAvailable.description: Whether a full resolver strategy trace (winner + losers + per-strategy reasoning) is persisted for this edge. Currently always `false` — the resolver discards loser attempts. Honest signal so consumers know this is edge-breadcrumb info, not a causal explanation.
